info.name: Sprauth
info.description:
Passwordless, post-quantum (ML-DSA-65) challenge-response authentication API.

Flow:
1. POST /challenge/init to get a server-signed challenge.
2. The collection pre-request script signs the decoded `challenge` with the embedded client ML-DSA-65 key (`clientSecretKey`) and fills `signature` / `clientPublicKey` / `identity` automatically — no external tooling needed.
3. POST /challenge/valid to check (and optionally consume) the challenge's pending status, or POST /session/auth (checks the proof and also mints access/refresh tokens).

Collection variables are used throughout so requests can be chained without manual copy/paste; several requests have test scripts that populate them automatically from responses.

exec: // Sprauth manual-testing crypto (ML-DSA-65). Runs before every request:
//  - signs `challengeString` into `signature` for Authenticate / Check Challenge Valid.
// The signing library is embedded as the `mldsaBundle` collection variable.
console.log('[sprauth] clientSecretKey not set - in-sandbox signing disabled.');
var b64ToBytes = function (b64) { var bin = atob(b64); var u = new Uint8Array(bin.length); for (var i = 0; i < bin.length; i++) u[i] = bin.charCodeAt(i); return u; };
var bytesToB64 = function (bytes) { var s = ''; for (var i = 0; i < bytes.length; i++) s += String.fromCharCode(bytes[i]); return btoa(s); };
var bytesToHex = function (bytes) { var h = ''; for (var i = 0; i < bytes.length; i++) { h += (bytes[i] >>> 4).toString(16); h += (bytes[i] & 15).toString(16); } return h; };

var utf8ToBytes = function (str) { var out = []; for (var i = 0; i < str.length; i++) { var c = str.charCodeAt(i); if (c < 128) { out.push(c); } else if (c < 2048) { out.push(192 | (c >> 6), 128 | (c & 63)); } else { out.push(224 | (c >> 12), 128 | ((c >> 6) & 63), 128 | (c & 63)); } } return new Uint8Array(out); };
var deriveAddress = function (pub) { var h = C.sha256(pub); return 'pqc1' + bytesToHex(h.slice(h.length - 20)); };
// Derive public key + identity once; cache keyed by a cheap fingerprint of the secret key.
// Local (highest-precedence) copies so a selected environment can't shadow these.
// Sign the current challenge for requests that submit a client proof-of-possession.
console.log('[sprauth] no challengeString yet - run Init Challenge first.');
console.log('[sprauth] pre-request crypto error: ' + (e && e.message ? e.message : e));

;__sprauthExports

request.description: Returns the server's ML-DSA-65 public key, base64-encoded. Used to verify server-signed tokens independently of this API.

Issues a server-signed, single-use challenge for `identity`. Stores `tokenId` in Redis with a TTL. Populates `challengeJwt`, `tokenId`, and `challengeString` collection variables on success.

`customClaims` is spread into the token payload before reserved fields (`iat`, `identity`, `intent`, `challenge`, `tokenId`), so it cannot override them.

Verifies a challenge the same way Authenticate does — the server checks the challenge JWT's own signature and the client's `signature` over the challenge string against the identity/public key — then reports whether the challenge is still pending (unconsumed) in Redis. The collection pre-request script signs `challengeString` and fills `signature`/`clientPublicKey` automatically — just run Init Challenge first. Set `consume` to `true` to atomically remove the challenge as part of the check (single use); leave `false` to just poll status (before Authenticate, to confirm it hasn't expired or already been used).

Verifies the server-signed challenge and the client's proof-of-possession signature over `challengeString`, then on success mints `accessToken` and `refreshToken` (self-issued, ML-DSA-signed) and starts a new Redis-backed session for the identity, returning its `sessionId`. Consumes the challenge (single use) regardless of outcome.

`signature` = ML-DSA-65 signature over the raw `challengeString` bytes, produced with the client's private key.
`publicKey` = the client's ML-DSA-65 public key (base64); its derived `pqc1...` address must match `identity`.

Verifies the server's own signature on `refreshToken` (rejecting anything that isn't a `tokenType: "refreshToken"` token), then checks that the session baked into the token is still valid in Redis (renewing its TTL). On success, mints and returns a fresh `accessToken`/`refreshToken` pair for the same session.
Lists all active session IDs for `identity`.

query.description: set to true to renew the session TTL on check

request.description: Checks whether a specific session is still valid, optionally renewing its TTL.

Ends a single session (e.g. logout on one device). Requires a valid access token as `Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>`; the identity is derived from the token, so only the caller's own sessions can be ended.

Ends all of the caller's sessions except the ones listed in `except` (e.g. "log out everywhere else"). Pass an empty array to end every session. Requires a valid access token as `Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>`; the identity is derived from the token.
Counts `identity`'s currently pending (unconsumed) challenges in Redis.
Counts every currently pending (unconsumed) challenge in Redis, across all identities.
Counts `identity`'s currently active sessions in Redis.
Counts every currently active session in Redis, across all identities.
